players.name: Vozinha
players.bio: Cabo Verde's legendary shot-stopper, Vozinha is the bedrock of the Tubarões Azuis. At 40, he arrives at his first World Cup as the nation's most-capped goalkeeper — proof that fine wine and shot-stopping age well.
players.facts: At 40, Vozinha is one of the oldest goalkeepers ever to appear at a FIFA World Cup.

players.name: Stopira
players.bio: A seasoned warrior at the back, Stopira has been marshalling Cabo Verde's defence for over a decade. The veteran centre-back brings calm authority and four international goals — not bad for a man paid to keep them out the other end.
players.birth_location: Nossa Senhora da Graça, Cape Verde
players.facts: Stopira has represented Cabo Verde across three AFCON campaigns, making him one of the squad's most decorated servants.

players.name: Diney
players.bio: Born in Tarrafal and now plying his trade in the UAE, Diney is a composed, physical centre-back who has become a reliable fixture in Cabo Verde's backline. His debut World Cup arrives as reward for consistent performances at club and country.
players.facts: Diney plays club football in the UAE Pro League — making him one of few Cape Verdeans based in the Arabian Gulf.

players.name: Roberto Lopes
players.bio: Born in Crumlin, Dublin, Roberto Lopes swapped a League of Ireland career with Shamrock Rovers for World Cup football. A commanding defender with an Irish accent and Cape Verdean heart, he's proof football's diaspora stories never get old.
players.facts: Roberto Lopes is believed to be the first player to reach a World Cup while playing in the League of Ireland.

players.name: Logan Costa
players.bio: Born in Saint-Denis and forged in French football, Logan Costa is Cabo Verde's most exciting defensive talent. The Villarreal centre-back is composed on the ball and fierce in the challenge — a future cornerstone of this squad.
players.facts: Logan Costa was born on April Fools' Day 2001. Opponents who underestimate him quickly realise the joke's on them.

players.name: Kevin Pina
players.full_name: Kevin Lenini Gonçalves Pereira de Pina
players.bio: A combative and energetic midfielder who breaks up play and drives forward from deep, Kevin Pina brings steel to Cabo Verde's engine room. His time in the Russian Premier League has sharpened his competitive edge.
players.facts: Kevin Pina plays his club football for Krasnodar in Russia — one of the more unexpected stops on any Cape Verdean's career map.

players.name: Jovane Cabral
players.bio: A pacy, direct winger with tricks to burn, Jovane Cabral is one of Cabo Verde's most exciting attackers. Born in Assomada and a product of Sporting CP's academy, he brings European pedigree to the island nation's World Cup adventure.
players.facts: Jovane Cabral came through Sporting CP's famed academy — the same production line that shaped Cristiano Ronaldo.

players.name: João Paulo
players.bio: A versatile and industrious midfielder, João Paulo brings energy and technical quality to Cabo Verde's midfield. His move to Romanian giants FCSB underlines his ambition, and this World Cup marks the pinnacle of his international journey.
players.facts: João Paulo plays for FCSB — the club once known as Steaua Bucharest, winners of the 1986 European Cup.

players.name: Gilson Benchimol
players.bio: Young, sharp, and hungry, Gilson Benchimol is one of Cabo Verde's brightest striking prospects. Six goals in 21 caps at just 24 is an impressive ratio, and the World Cup stage offers the perfect platform to announce himself globally.
players.facts: Gilson Benchimol was born on 29 December 2001 — he almost missed being a 2001 baby entirely.

players.name: Jamiro Monteiro
players.bio: The heartbeat of Cabo Verde's midfield, Rotterdam-born Jamiro Monteiro is a dynamic box-to-box operator with vision and tenacity. Wearing the iconic No.10, he arrives at his first World Cup as one of the squad's most experienced midfielders.
players.facts: Jamiro Monteiro previously played in MLS for Philadelphia Union, making him a familiar face to American World Cup audiences.

players.name: Garry Rodrigues
players.bio: Garry Rodrigues is Cabo Verde's most prolific wide man — a rapid, direct winger who has tormented defences across Europe. With 10 international goals from the flank, he's the kind of player who makes defenders check their insurance.
players.facts: Garry Rodrigues once played in the Turkish Süper Lig for Galatasaray, where he scored in the UEFA Europa League.

players.name: Márcio Rosa
players.bio: Márcio Rosa is the reliable understudy to Vozinha, a goalkeeper who has quietly built his international profile across 11 caps. Based in Bulgaria with Montana, he's ready to answer the call should duty come knocking.
players.facts: Márcio Rosa plays in the Bulgarian top flight — one of Cabo Verde's most geographically adventurous club postings.

players.name: Sidny Lopes Cabral
players.bio: A right-back with an eye for goal, Rotterdam-born Sidny Lopes Cabral has made an explosive start to his international career — three goals in just 11 caps is a remarkable return for a defender. Benfica's gain is Cabo Verde's delight.
players.facts: Sidny has scored 3 international goals in just 11 caps — an attacking return that would embarrass some strikers.

players.name: Deroy Duarte
players.bio: Cool, composed and technically assured, Deroy Duarte is a central midfielder who dictates tempo with quiet authority. The Ludogorets man was born in Rotterdam and has become an important cog in Cabo Verde's midfield machine.
players.facts: Deroy Duarte was born on 4 July — American Independence Day — but he saved his fireworks for Bulgarian football.

players.name: Laros Duarte
players.bio: A tenacious defensive midfielder with a knack for reading the game, Laros Duarte provides the Tubarões Azuis with vital protection at the base of midfield. His journey from Rotterdam to the World Cup stage is the stuff of football fairytales.
players.facts: Laros and Deroy Duarte share a surname and both hail from Rotterdam — but they are not brothers. Probably.

players.name: Yannick Semedo
players.bio: A creative central midfielder with an eye for the spectacular, Yannick Semedo adds flair and ambition to Cabo Verde's midfield. Born in Praia and now performing in the Portuguese Primeira Liga with Farense, he's earned his World Cup place.
players.facts: Yannick Semedo shares his surname with teammate Willy Semedo — no relation, just a very popular Cape Verdean name.

players.name: Willy Semedo
players.bio: Born in Bordeaux, Willy Semedo is a pacy left winger who has built a solid international career across 38 caps. His ability to carry the ball at pace and deliver in the final third makes him a constant danger on Cabo Verde's left flank.
players.facts: Willy Semedo is one of three Semedos in the Cape Verde squad. The opposition's job of marking them is already confusing.

players.name: Telmo Arcanjo
players.bio: A lively right winger with quick feet and an eye for goal, Telmo Arcanjo has emerged as one of Portuguese football's more exciting young talents at Vitória de Guimarães. Born in Lisbon, his World Cup debut is a richly deserved milestone.
players.facts: Telmo Arcanjo was born in Lisbon but represents the islands — a classic example of Cabo Verde's remarkable diaspora squad.

players.name: Dailon Livramento
players.bio: Dailon Livramento is Cabo Verde's most lethal striker — seven goals in 22 caps is an outstanding return for a 25-year-old. The Casa Pia forward combines intelligent movement with a clinical finish, and will fancy his chances on the World Cup stage.
players.facts: Dailon Livramento averages a goal every 3.1 caps — a ratio that puts him among Cabo Verde's all-time sharpest strikers.

players.name: Ryan Mendes
players.bio: Ryan Mendes is a Cabo Verde legend — nearly 100 caps and 22 goals from the forward line over a remarkable international career. Born on Fogo island, he's a volcanic presence in attack and the most experienced outfield player in the squad.
players.facts: Ryan Mendes has 98 international caps — if he reaches 100, he'll join a very exclusive Cape Verdean century club.

players.name: Nuno da Costa
players.bio: A tall, experienced centre-forward who leads the line with physicality and hold-up play, Nuno da Costa brings veteran know-how to Cabo Verde's attack. His time at İstanbul Başakşehir adds Turkish Süper Lig nous to his considerable CV.
players.facts: Nuno da Costa has played professional football in France, Switzerland, and Turkey — a well-travelled striker indeed.

players.name: Steven Moreira
players.bio: A dependable right-back with experience of MLS football at Columbus Crew, Steven Moreira brings defensive solidity and athleticism to Cabo Verde's backline. Born in France, the World Cup on American soil feels particularly fitting for him.
players.facts: Steven Moreira plays for Columbus Crew in MLS — meaning he's already very familiar with World Cup host nation USA.

players.name: CJ dos Santos
players.bio: Born in Monterrey, Mexico, CJ dos Santos is one of the most uniquely placed players at this World Cup — a Cape Verdean representing a nation he may never have visited, playing a tournament in the country of his birth. Football is wonderfully strange.
players.facts: CJ dos Santos was born in Mexico and plays the World Cup there — he might be the ultimate home-and-away player simultaneously.

players.name: Wagner Pina
players.bio: A young, attack-minded right-back with the energy to bomb forward all day, Wagner Pina has earned his Trabzonspor stripes and is making early inroads into the Cabo Verde setup. Born in Lisbon, he's one of the squad's brightest emerging talents.
players.facts: At 23, Wagner Pina plays in the Turkish Süper Lig for Trabzonspor — one of Turkey's most passionate and demanding clubs.

players.name: Kelvin Pires
players.bio: A centre-back born in Mindelo — the cultural capital of Cabo Verde — Kelvin Pires represents the home islands with pride. Despite only six caps, he's already weighed in with a goal and is part of the next generation building this squad's future.
players.facts: Kelvin Pires plays for SJK in Finland's Veikkausliga — the northernmost league represented in Cabo Verde's World Cup squad.

players.name: Hélio Varela
players.bio: A tricky, creative left winger with a football brain to match his quick feet, Hélio Varela has made his mark in Israeli football with Maccabi Tel Aviv. Born in Almada, Portugal, he brings European quality and youthful ambition to the Tubarões Azuis.
players.facts: Hélio Varela plays for Maccabi Tel Aviv — one of the most storied clubs in Israeli football history, founded in 1936.
